name: Retune PTX Hot-Band Grouped Rows From 4 Down To 2 On The 3-Stage Surface

summary.name: Retune PTX Hot-Band Grouped Rows From 4 Down To 2 On The 3-Stage Surface
summary.hypothesis: The current 3-stage run already removed long_scoreboard as the dominant tax, so the remaining regression is mostly handoff depth: barrier is up by 1.25 pts and mio_throttle is up by 0.59 while active warps stay flat. Reducing PTX grouped rows from 4 to 2 is the smallest way to shorten CTA group depth and reduce synchronization pressure without giving back the 3-stage latency-hiding benefit or touching the active PTX dispatch.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Grouped-row batching is likely over-amortizing locality on the 3-stage surface and paying extra barrier plus handoff delay per CTA group.
summary.risk: Low-moderate. This is a one-constant launch-order retune on the current winning kernel body, but smaller CTA groups can still lose locality if the batching cut is too aggressive.
ranking_notes: This is the cheapest single-variable probe that targets the exact regressions introduced by the 3-stage run: barrier and mio_throttle.
It keeps the long_scoreboard win from the 3-stage surface instead of throwing that information away immediately.
If it loses, the result cleanly tells us whether the remaining tax is in CTA group depth or in the late-drain seam itself.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if grouped_rows=2 materially increases DRAM or L2 pressure without reducing barrier or mio_throttle, or if it forces a broader dispatch-family rewrite.
expected_local_changes: Do not touch launch bounds, stage count, or the accumulator schedule in the first pass.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
